d3s_e1_cost_crisis.speaker: Mentor
d3s_e1_cost_crisis.text: Day 3 and cash is tight. Your laundromat is struggling. You need to make smart financial decisions today or the business could be in real trouble.

d3s_e1_cost_b.speaker: You
d3s_e1_cost_b.text: Bills are piling up, and revenue is lower than expected. I need to cut costs somewhere without destroying what makes this place special.

choices.text: Reduce staff hours temporarily
Use cheaper detergent to save costs
Launch a flash sale to generate quick cash
Apply for a small business emergency loan

d3s_e1_staff.speaker: Staff
d3s_e1_staff.text: Reduced hours mean reduced pay. We understand the business is struggling, but this puts real pressure on us personally.

d3s_e1_quality.speaker: Regular Customer
d3s_e1_quality.text: Something's different. The detergent smells cheaper. I hope standards aren't dropping.

d3s_e1_sale.speaker: Customer
d3s_e1_sale.text: A flash sale? Perfect timing! I brought three friends along. You should do these more often!

d3s_e1_loan.speaker: Bank Officer
d3s_e1_loan.text: Given your short operating history, the loan comes with higher interest. Funds are available within 24 hours.

d3s_e1_result_staff.speaker: Mentor
d3s_e1_result_staff.text: Cost cutting through labor is a double-edged sword. It preserves cash but damages the team trust that carries you through hard times.

d3s_e1_result_quality.speaker: Mentor
d3s_e1_result_quality.text: Quality cuts are visible to loyal customers immediately. Short-term savings can trigger long-term reputation damage.

d3s_e1_result_sale.speaker: Mentor
d3s_e1_result_sale.text: Flash sales generate immediate cash flow and foot traffic. Creative revenue generation beats cost-cutting when protecting culture matters.

d3s_e1_result_loan.speaker: Mentor
d3s_e1_result_loan.text: Debt financing buys time but adds future pressure. Use borrowed capital only for revenue-generating activities.

d3s_r_supplier_pressure.speaker: Supplier
d3s_r_supplier_pressure.text: I've noticed your orders are smaller lately. If you can't maintain volume, I'll have to increase your prices.

d3s_r_supplier_b.speaker: You
d3s_r_supplier_b.text: The supplier is threatening higher prices. How do you respond?

choices.text: Explain your situation and ask for patience
Accept the price increase
Look for a cheaper supplier
Promise to increase volume next month

d3s_r_supplier_honest.speaker: Supplier
d3s_r_supplier_honest.text: I appreciate your honesty. I'll give you two months at current prices to turn things around.

d3s_r_supplier_switch.speaker: New Supplier
d3s_r_supplier_switch.text: We can supply you, but quality is lower. You get what you pay for.

d3s_r_supplier_promise.speaker: Supplier
d3s_r_supplier_promise.text: I'll hold you to that. One month grace period.

d3s_r_supplier_result_honest.speaker: Mentor
d3s_r_supplier_result_honest.text: Transparency in tough times builds goodwill. People are more understanding than you think when you're honest.

d3s_r_supplier_result_accept.speaker: Mentor
d3s_r_supplier_result_accept.text: Accepting higher costs without negotiation hurts your margins. Always try to negotiate first.

d3s_r_supplier_result_switch.speaker: Mentor
d3s_r_supplier_result_switch.text: Cheaper suppliers often mean lower quality. Make sure you're not saving money at the expense of your reputation.

d3s_r_supplier_result_promise.speaker: Mentor
d3s_r_supplier_result_promise.text: Promises create pressure to deliver. Make sure you can keep them before committing.

d3s_r_bad_review_viral.speaker: Mentor
d3s_r_bad_review_viral.text: A customer left a scathing online review about your laundromat. It's gaining traction.

d3s_r_review_b.speaker: You
d3s_r_review_b.text: The review complains about dirty machines and poor service. How do you respond?

choices.text: Respond publicly with an apology
Message them privately to resolve
Ignore it — hope it goes away
Ask happy customers to leave reviews

d3s_r_review_public.speaker: Online User
d3s_r_review_public.text: The owner actually responded and apologized. Maybe it's worth another chance.

d3s_r_review_private.speaker: Reviewer
d3s_r_review_private.text: A personal message and an apology? I'll update my review.

d3s_r_review_ignore.speaker: Potential Customer
d3s_r_review_ignore.text: They never even replied. Doesn't say much about their customer service.

d3s_r_review_positive.speaker: Happy Customer
d3s_r_review_positive.text: Of course! You've been great to us. Happy to share my experience!

d3s_r_review_result_public.speaker: Mentor
d3s_r_review_result_public.text: Public responses show accountability. Others see how you handle criticism — that builds trust over time.

d3s_r_review_result_private.speaker: Mentor
d3s_r_review_result_private.text: Personal outreach shows genuine care. Turning a critic into a fan is one of the best outcomes.

d3s_r_review_result_ignore.speaker: Mentor
d3s_r_review_result_ignore.text: Silence reads as indifference online. Unaddressed negative reviews compound over time.

d3s_r_review_result_positive.speaker: Mentor
d3s_r_review_result_positive.text: Amplifying positive voices is smart. It balances the narrative while you work on improvements.

d3s_r_theft.speaker: Staff
d3s_r_theft.text: Boss! Someone broke into the coin machine overnight. We lost a week's worth of coins, and the machine is damaged.

d3s_r_theft_b.speaker: You
d3s_r_theft_b.text: A theft and damaged machine. Other customers are watching how you handle this.

choices.text: Call the police immediately
Repair the machine quietly
Post a warning sign and add cameras
Do nothing — hope it doesn't happen again

d3s_r_theft_police.speaker: Customer
d3s_r_theft_police.text: Good that they called the police. At least they take security seriously.

d3s_r_theft_quiet.speaker: Customer
d3s_r_theft_quiet.text: Did something happen? They're being weird about that machine.

d3s_r_theft_security.speaker: Customer
d3s_r_theft_security.text: New cameras and signs? They're taking safety seriously now.

d3s_r_theft_nothing.speaker: Customer
d3s_r_theft_nothing.text: That broken machine has been like that all day. Don't they care?

d3s_r_theft_result_police.speaker: Mentor
d3s_r_theft_result_police.text: Calling authorities shows you take security seriously. Transparency builds trust even in hard times.

d3s_r_theft_result_quiet.speaker: Mentor
d3s_r_theft_result_quiet.text: Hiding problems doesn't make them disappear. Customers notice when something's off.

d3s_r_theft_result_security.speaker: Mentor
d3s_r_theft_result_security.text: Visible security measures reassure customers. Proactive protection is good business even when money is tight.

d3s_r_theft_result_nothing.speaker: Mentor
d3s_r_theft_result_nothing.text: Inaction during a crisis erodes trust. Customers want to feel safe in your business.

d3s_e3_accountant_finances.speaker: Mentor
d3s_e3_accountant_finances.text: A difficult conversation is needed. Your accountant has reviewed the books, and the numbers are concerning.

d3s_e3_accountant_b.speaker: Accountant
d3s_e3_accountant_b.text: At your current burn rate, you have about three weeks of runway left. You need to increase revenue by 30% or cut costs by 20%. What's your plan?

choices.text: Focus entirely on revenue growth
Implement strict cost cutting
Do both — moderate cuts and growth
Seek a business mentor for help

d3s_e3_revenue.speaker: Accountant
d3s_e3_revenue.text: Aggressive revenue focus is high-risk, high-reward. If it works, you come out stronger.

d3s_e3_costs.speaker: Accountant
d3s_e3_costs.text: Cost cutting extends runway but doesn't solve the revenue problem. Don't cut things that generate income.

d3s_e3_both.speaker: Accountant
d3s_e3_both.text: A balanced approach is pragmatic. Neither fully solves the problem, but together they buy you time.

d3s_e3_mentor.speaker: Business Mentor
d3s_e3_mentor.text: Smart move seeking help! I've seen this before. Three specific changes can turn this around within two weeks.

d3s_e3_result_revenue.speaker: Mentor
d3s_e3_result_revenue.text: Betting on growth during a cash crisis takes courage. It can work but requires flawless execution under pressure.

d3s_e3_result_costs.speaker: Mentor
d3s_e3_result_costs.text: Cost control without revenue growth is a slow decline. Use the time you buy to build revenue.

d3s_e3_result_both.speaker: Mentor
d3s_e3_result_both.text: Balance under pressure is mature financial thinking. You're moving in the right direction on multiple fronts.

d3s_e3_result_mentor.speaker: Mentor
d3s_e3_result_mentor.text: Knowing when to ask for help is one of the most underrated business skills. Pride kills more businesses than bad markets do.

d3s_e4_staff_morale.speaker: Mentor
d3s_e4_staff_morale.text: Despite the financial pressure, your team has been incredible. But morale is suffering.

d3s_e4_staff_b.speaker: Staff
d3s_e4_staff_b.text: We know things are tight, boss. We're still here and still giving our best. We just want to know you see us.

choices.text: Express genuine heartfelt gratitude
Promise better times with a specific plan
Offer a small gesture — team meal on you
Be honest about how serious it is

d3s_e4_gratitude.speaker: Staff
d3s_e4_gratitude.text: That genuinely means a lot. Sometimes just being seen is enough to keep going.

d3s_e4_promise.speaker: Staff
d3s_e4_promise.text: A specific plan gives us something to hold on to. We can handle hard times when we see a path forward.

d3s_e4_meal.speaker: Staff
d3s_e4_meal.text: A team meal during tough times! It's such a small thing but means so much.

d3s_e4_honest.speaker: Staff
d3s_e4_honest.text: We appreciate you being straight with us. It's scary, but we'd rather know the truth.

d3s_e4_result_gratitude.speaker: Mentor
d3s_e4_result_gratitude.text: Authentic appreciation costs nothing but means everything. The best leaders make their people feel valued, especially when resources are scarce.

d3s_e4_result_promise.speaker: Mentor
d3s_e4_result_promise.text: Hope with a plan is leadership. Hope without a plan is just wishful thinking.

d3s_e4_result_meal.speaker: Mentor
d3s_e4_result_meal.text: Small gestures during hard times have disproportionate impact. The memory of being cared for outlasts any bonus.

d3s_e4_result_honest.speaker: Mentor
d3s_e4_result_honest.text: Radical transparency builds deep trust. Teams that face hard truths together develop resilience.

d3s_e5_recovery_plan.speaker: Mentor
d3s_e5_recovery_plan.text: End of day 3. Despite the struggles, you've made it through. Now you need a recovery plan.

d3s_e5_plan_b.speaker: You
d3s_e5_plan_b.text: Tomorrow is a new day. What's your focus for recovery?

choices.text: Focus on customer retention
Focus on cost reduction
Focus on team motivation
Focus on marketing and promotion

d3s_e5_retention.speaker: Regular Customer
d3s_e5_retention.text: I noticed you really trying today. I'll stick with you.

d3s_e5_costs.speaker: Mentor
d3s_e5_costs.text: Cost control buys time. Use that time wisely to rebuild revenue.

d3s_e5_team.speaker: Staff
d3s_e5_team.text: Thank you for believing in us. We've got your back, boss.

d3s_e5_marketing.speaker: New Customer
d3s_e5_marketing.text: I came because of the promotion. This place is great!

d3s_e5_result_retention.speaker: Mentor
d3s_e5_result_retention.text: Keeping existing customers is cheaper than finding new ones. Smart focus during hard times.

d3s_e5_result_costs.speaker: Mentor
d3s_e5_result_costs.text: Cost cutting without revenue growth is a slow death. Make sure you're also building income.

d3s_e5_result_team.speaker: Mentor
d3s_e5_result_team.text: Your team is your greatest asset. Investing in them during tough times builds loyalty that pays back tenfold.

d3s_e5_result_marketing.speaker: Mentor
d3s_e5_result_marketing.text: Marketing during a downturn is bold. New customers now could become regulars for years.
